page: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. The system delivers 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory operations. The server achieves top-10% scores in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU metrics, and Redis SET operations, which reach 184.73 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is also in the top tier, though uncached large-block read and write speeds drop to the top 25% tier. Single-core performance, as measured by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, sits in the average to top-tier range, reflecting a design optimized for parallelized throughput rather than raw single-threaded speed.

From a resource density perspective, the c6a.metal offers high compute density with its 192 dedicated vCPUs, making it a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, reliance on DDR4 memory, and average single-threaded decompression speeds. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and parallelized database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. It features 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the c6a.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, PassMark scores, and Redis SET throughput, alongside strong software compilation and ray tracing capabilities. However, single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth fall into average to strong tiers. This server is optimized for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations where local storage is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and high-throughput multi-core processing for parallelized enterprise workloads.
